title: fagara mantchurica var. subinermis
intro: Fagara mantchurica var. subinermis, also known as the Korean prickly ash, is a type of plant that belongs to the Zanthoxylum genus, which is a group of deciduous shrubs or small trees native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve pain, and reduce inflammation, due to the presence of compounds like caryophyllene and caryophyllene oxide. The health benefits of Fagara mantchurica var. subinermis include its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Fagara mantchurica var. subinermis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried roots and stems being commonly used in traditional herbal medicine.
